doc_content.summary: stark-phase-execute is a fully autonomous execution engine that takes a development plan and implements it end-to-end. It automatically fetches (or decomposes) tasks, runs an implementation loop for each issue including semantic commits, PR creation, multi-agent review with autonomous fix rounds, and immediate merging. Post-execution, it handles regression testing, semantic versioning, deployment, and performance logging without any user intervention.
key_features: Zero-intervention task implementation loop (checkout -> implement -> test -> commit).
Auto-decomposition of .md plans into GitHub issues if no tasks currently exist.
Integrated multi-agent PR review process with autonomous, isolated fix rounds.
Automatic end-of-phase regression testing, CHANGELOG generation, and semantic release.
Post-execution dashboard highlighting success rates, agent scorecards, and prompt improvement flags.

alt_text: A user-focused flowchart and UI visualization of the stark-phase-execute workflow, detailing the autonomous lifecycle from initialization and task fetching to the implementation subagent loop, multi-agent review, merging, release, and final dashboard generation.
